perturbation.original_text: 2.7 "Sponsorship Rights" means the bundle of rights, services and deliverables Allied will provide to Newegg as set out in Schedule 4, which includes the license of, and rights with respect to, Allied Marks granted in Section 5.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.7 "Sponsorship Rights" means the bundle of rights, services and deliverables Allied will provide to Newegg as set out in Schedule 4, which explicitly excludes any license of, and rights with respect to, Allied Marks granted in Section 5.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states Sponsorship Rights *includes* the license of Allied Marks. The modified text states it *excludes* it. This creates a direct contradiction about what Newegg receives as part of the Sponsorship Rights and makes the enforcement uncertain. This changes the scope of services, which could lead to legal disputes between the entities involved.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Allied shall provide, by the license granted in Section 5.1 and otherwise as appropriate, to or for the benefit of Newegg, the Sponsorship Rights, including generally providing advertising space in all of Allied's media and participation in Allied's marketing activities relating to the Arena
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 2.7 explicitly excludes the license of Allied Marks from the Sponsorship Rights, directly contradicting Section 4.1, which states that Allied shall provide the Sponsorship Rights, including the license granted in Section 5.1. This creates a clear and undeniable contradiction regarding whether the license of Allied Marks is included in the Sponsorship Rights.

perturbation.original_text: 12.1 Each Party ("Indemnitor") will defend, indemnify and hold the other Party (including associated officers, directors, shareholders, employees, agents and affiliates) (cumulatively, "Indemnitee") harmless from and against any and all losses, damages, claims, liabilities and expenses (including reasonable legal fees), suffered or incurred as a result of or in connection with any claim, suit, action, demand, or proceeding brought against Indemnitee based upon (a) a claim of a failure to perform, or a breach by Indemnitor of, any obligation, warranty, representation or covenant in this Agreement; (b) a claim of personal injury or property damage arising out of the fault or negligence of Indemnitor, its representatives, agents, or employees; or (c) a claim of infringement or misappropriation of any patent, trademark, copyright or other proprietary right held by any third party.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.1 Each Party ("Indemnitor") may, but is not obligated to, defend, indemnify and hold the other Party (including associated officers, directors, shareholders, employees, agents and affiliates) (cumulatively, "Indemnitee") harmless from and against any and all losses, damages, claims, liabilities and expenses (excluding legal fees), suffered or incurred as a result of or in connection with any claim, suit, action, demand, or proceeding brought against Indemnitee based upon (a) a claim of a failure to perform, or a breach by Indemnitor of, any obligation, warranty, representation or covenant in this Agreement; (b) a claim of personal injury or property damage arising out of the fault or negligence of Indemnitor, its representatives, agents, or employees; or (c) a claim of infringement or misappropriation of any patent, trademark, copyright or other proprietary right held by any third party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the Indemnitor 'will' defend and indemnify the other party, indicating a mandatory obligation. By changing 'will' to 'may, but is not obligated to', the modified text introduces uncertainty regarding the Indemnitor's duty. Additionally, excluding legal fees directly contradicts the previous statement about 'reasonable legal fees', creating an in-text contradiction. This affects the enforceability and scope of the indemnification clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15.10 Governing Law and Jurisdiction. Without reference to choice or conflict of law principles, this Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, USA. The Parties unconditionally submit to exclusive jurisdiction of and accept as the exclusive venue for any legal proceeding involving this Agreement the state and federal courts located in the County of Los Angeles, California. Before any Party (the "Complaining Party") may bring any legal proceeding against the other (the "Non Complaining Party"), the Complaining Party shall first make a reasonable and good faith attempt to resolve all disputes privately by notifying and providing to the Non Complaining Party of the Complaining Party's complaints, reasons and supporting evidence for the complaints, and the reasonable steps Complaining Party would like the Non Complaining Party to take in order to address the complaints. If for any reason the Non-Complaining Party disagrees with either the complaint or the steps suggested to address the complaints, the Parties shall discuss and work on an amicable solution for at least thirty (30) days before the Complaining Party may bring any legal proceeding to resolve the complaints. Any dispute, claim or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the breach, termination, enforcement, interpretation, or validity thereof, including the determination of the scope and applicability of this agreement to arbitrate, shall be determined by arbitration in Los Angeles County, California, by an arbitrator of JAMS, in accordance with its arbitration rules and procedures then in effect. Judgment on the arbitrator's award may be entered in any court having jurisdiction. The prevailing Party in any dispute involving this Agreement shall be entitled to recover from the other Party its costs, expenses, and reasonable attorneys' fees (including any fees for expert witnesses, paralegals, or other legal service providers). This Section 15.10 shall not preclude or place any condition on any Party from seeking injunctive relief from a court of appropriate jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 12.1, by changing 'will' to 'may, but is not obligated to' regarding indemnification and excluding legal fees, directly contradicts Section 15.10. Section 15.10 states that 'The prevailing Party in any dispute involving this Agreement shall be entitled to recover from the other Party its costs, expenses, and reasonable attorneys' fees'. By removing the mandatory obligation to defend and indemnify and excluding legal fees in Section 12.1, a direct conflict arises regarding the recovery of attorneys' fees, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6.3 Allied shall use the Newegg Marks only in strict compliance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement. Allied's use of the Newegg Marks (a) shall be subject to Newegg's right of review and approval, and prior direction and control, to be exercised in Newegg's sole discretion, and (b) shall, at all times, meet or exceed Newegg's trademark-usage guidelines and quality standards which may be provided by Newegg from time to time ("Acceptable Quality Standards"). Without limiting any other provision of this Section 6.3, if at any time Newegg reasonably determines that Allied's use of the Newegg Marks fails to comply with this Agreement or to conform to the Acceptable Quality Standards, Allied shall, within five (5) days of receipt of notice from Newegg, correct its use of the Newegg Marks so that its use is in compliance with this Agreement and the Acceptable Quality Standards or cease using, and remove, the Newegg Marks from all of Allied's videos, streams and other publications in all media ("Allied's Correction Action"). Allied's obligation to take and complete Allied's Correction Action shall survive any expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.3 Allied shall use the Newegg Marks only in strict compliance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement. Allied's use of the Newegg Marks (a) shall be subject to Allied's discretion, without the need for Newegg's right of review and approval, or direction and control, and (b) shall, at all times, meet Allied's own trademark-usage guidelines and quality standards, irrespective of the Acceptable Quality Standards provided by Newegg. Without limiting any other provision of this Section 6.3, if at any time Newegg reasonably determines that Allied's use of the Newegg Marks fails to comply with this Agreement or to conform to the Acceptable Quality Standards, Newegg may, but is not obligated to, notify Allied. Allied's obligation to take and complete Allied's Correction Action shall not survive any expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text gives Newegg sole discretion over the use of its trademarks. The altered text gives Allied the discretion, directly contradicting Newegg's control. Additionally, it removes Newegg's obligation to notify Allied of issues, making 'Allied's Correction Action' non-enforceable after the deal ends, which introduces uncertainty. The original intent was for Newegg to retain control and oversight, but that has been completely inverted, making it likely to create conflicts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 Allied acknowledges and agrees that Newegg has valuable goodwill and reputation in the Newegg Marks and that Newegg is and shall be at all times the sole and exclusive owner of rights, including Intellectual Property Rights, in and related to the Newegg Marks. Allied does not acquire any right, title, or interest in or to the Newegg Marks by virtue of the limited license granted in Section 6.1, or through Allied's permitted use of the Newegg Marks, other than the right to use such Newegg Marks in accordance with that license. Allied acknowledges that its use of the Newegg Marks pursuant to this Agreement, and all goodwill associated with such use, shall inure exclusively to the benefit of Newegg. Allied further acknowledges and agrees that Newegg shall have sole control and final editorial say, in Newegg's sole discretion, over the marketing/promotion, appearance, design, layout, placement, and presentation of Newegg's Products, including all packaging, advertisements and other marketing and promotional materials relating to the Newegg Products.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 6.3 directly contradicts section 6.2. Section 6.2 clearly states that Newegg has sole control over the Newegg Marks and final editorial say, while the changed section 6.3 gives Allied discretion over the use of the marks, removing Newegg's right of review and approval. This creates a direct conflict regarding who controls the trademarks, which is a core element of the agreement and a strong contradiction. Additionally, the changed text states that Allied's obligation to take and complete Allied's Correction Action shall not survive any expiration or termination of this Agreement, which directly contradicts the original text.
